title: Units and GPA
content: To earn a Berkeley bachelor's degree, students must complete at least 120 semester units and maintain at least a 2.0 cumulative GPA. The senior residence rule requires at least 24 of the remaining units after reaching 90 total units to be completed in residence over at least two semesters, with at least 6 units in each semester.

title: Grades
content: Berkeley's grades policy defines A through D grades as passing, with D-, D, and D+ described as barely passing work. F indicates failure and earns no units. Grade symbols and grade-point values are governed by the catalog grades page.

title: Courses and Units
content: The courses-and-units policy explains how repeated Berkeley coursework is recorded. When a course is repeated under Berkeley rules, both enrollments remain on the transcript, but the most recently earned grade and units are used in the GPA calculation for the repeated course.

title: American Cultures Requirement
content: All Berkeley undergraduates must complete one approved American Cultures course. The catalog treats this as a university-wide campus requirement that may be completed through an approved course in any term before graduation.

title: Entry-Level Writing and Reading and Composition Requirements
content: Berkeley's entry-level writing and reading-and-composition policy requires students to satisfy the campus writing expectation and the two-part reading and composition sequence. This requirement sits alongside major requirements and is not waived by completing CS major courses alone.

title: American History and Institutions Requirement
content: The American History and Institutions requirement is a separate campus requirement that must be satisfied in addition to the CS major. Berkeley catalogs this requirement with other university and campus graduation rules.
